metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch

bypass.reason: False positive: send_email_direct is a function alias assigned at module level, not a constant
False positive: trinity_dir, required_checks, optional_checks are local variables inside functions, not module-level constants
False positive: all_files, last_activity are local variables inside functions, not module-level constants
False positive: result, time_diff, counts are local variables inside functions, not module-level constants
False positive: red_flags, activity, health_data are local variables inside functions, not module-level constants
False positive: load_registry, is_action_due, update_last_run are function references from try/except import fallback, not constants
Entry point script — lives in apps/ root by design, not a module or handler
Branch entry point — lives in apps/ root by design per AIPass convention
Public API for plugin discovery — used by scheduler_cron and available for external callers. Referenced in help text.

Authorized cross-branch wake_branch import (DPLAN-0204 §2.8 path A). ai_mail exposes wake_branch only via its handler — ai_mail's own modules and @trigger import it identically; no module entry point exists to use. Root fix (an ai_mail module wrapper) is tracked separately.
Authorized cross-branch import (TDPLAN-0008 contract). daemon emits lifecycle pings via skills telegram notifier — the only send path; no module wrapper exists.
Authorized cross-branch import (TDPLAN-0008 contract). send_telegram_notification lives in @skills handler — daemon's notifier wraps it fail-soft.
drone @daemon run with no args runs one scheduler tick — that IS its primary function. A no-args introspection gate would break the core invocation (same pattern as update.py, activity_report.py).
activity, activity-report, activity_report commands all work with no args (default 24h). No-args introspection gate would break valid no-arg invocations.
update runs the status digest with no args — that IS its primary function. Showing introspection instead was reported as a dead-end UX bug (DPLAN-0085).
Scheduler plugin — autodiscovered by plugins/__init__.py discover_plugins(). Lives in apps/plugins/ by design, not a module or handler.
Scheduler plugin — autodiscovered by plugins/__init__.py discover_plugins(). Lives in apps/plugins/ by design, not a module or handler.
Scheduler plugin — autodiscovered by plugins/__init__.py discover_plugins(). Lives in apps/plugins/ by design, not a module or handler.
Test file — lives in tests/ by convention, not in 3-layer apps/ structure
Test file — lives in tests/ by convention, not in 3-layer apps/ structure
Test methods are self-documenting via descriptive names — pytest convention
Test file — lives in tests/ by convention, not in 3-layer apps/ structure
Test file imports handlers directly to unit-test them — standard pytest pattern
Bare 'queue' renders the operational Rich table (td-47). Introspection gate would break the primary use case — users expect the job table, not module metadata.
install-timer/uninstall-timer are action commands — no-args IS the action (install). Introspection gate would break primary use case (same pattern as run.py).
Installer module — mkdir for ~/.config/systemd/user/ is the core purpose. Direct file ops are inherent to the install function.

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

example.reason: Circular dependency - logger cannot import CLI

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, imports, naming, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass
